title: Check freshness against an explicit SLA
description: Assert that the newest data is within an agreed recency window, because a pipeline can succeed while serving stale data.
body: Use when downstream decisions depend on recent data. Do not equate a green pipeline run with fresh data — a job can complete having processed nothing new, or an upstream can stall while the loader reports success. Actions: assert max(event/ingest timestamp) or latest-partition age is within the SLA, and alert when it is exceeded. Validation signals include a freshness check confirming the latest partition is within window and an alert firing when data goes stale. Risk boundary: stale data presented as current drives wrong decisions silently; treat freshness as a first-class check, separate from job success.
